CHANGE RECORD 
When the quality of the products change s, the QuID is updated and a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections  or sub-sections have been updated.   The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies. 
 
Issue Date § Description of Change Author Validated By 
1.0 23/07/2018 All Creation of the document Gilles Garric  
 
 
 
 
Product name:  
ARCTIC_OMI_TEMPSAL_FWC  
 
Dataset name:  arctic_omi_tempsal_fwc 
 
Indicator evaluation method:  
Arctic liquid Freshwater Content (FWC in meters) is defined as the 
integrated differences of the measured salinity and a reference salinity 
from the surface to the bottom per unit area in the Arctic region with a 
water depth greater than 500m as: 
𝐹𝑊 = ∫ 𝑆𝑟𝑒𝑓 −  𝑆(𝑧)
𝑆𝑟𝑒𝑓
0
𝑏𝑜𝑡𝑡𝑜𝑚
𝑑𝑧 
where S is salinity, dz the vertical cell thickness of the dataset and Sref 
the salinity reference  set to 34.8 . Monthly means va lues are given here. 
Waters saltier than the salinity  reference are not included in the 
estimation.  
Area averaged time series for Arctic Ocean freshwater  content and their 
error bars are calculated for the Arctic region with a water depth greater 
than 500m.  
QUID for CMEMS OMIs: Arctic Ocean Freshwater Content

Scientific validation: 
More details and full scientific evaluation can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State Report #2 
(Garric and Hernandez, 2018). 
The quality evaluation of ARCTIC_OMI_FW C_area_averaged_anomalies is based on the 
“multi-product” approach as introduced in the second issue of the Oce an State Report 
(CMEMS OSR, 2018). Three  global products have been used to build an ensemble mean, and 
its associated ensemble spread (Figure 1, dark red line and orange shaded area). Results of Fig. 
1 indicate how each member is distributed around the ensemble mean.  
 
 
 
Figure 1: Monthly time series of area averaged ocean freshwater content over the specified Arctic 
regior. Time series are based on different data products, i.e. global ocean reanalysis GLORYS (dark 
gray), C-GLORS (blue), ORAS5 (light blue) and the ensemble mean (dark red) and the ensemble spread 
(orange shaded). Details on the products are given in the corresponding PUM for this OMI.  
 
Product used for indicator evaluation: 
Three global reanalyses  products, GLORYS, C -GLORS, ORAS5, have been used for the 
evaluation of area averaged (Arctic) ocean freshwater content. Details on the products are 
delivered in the PUM of this OMI. 
 
References: 
Garric and Hernandez , 2018: Arctic Ocean freshwater content. In: The Copernicus Marine 
Environment Monitoring Service Ocean State Report, issue 2, Journal of Operational 
Oceanography, under revision.

catalog_entry.summary: This document describes the Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI) for Arctic Ocean Freshwater Content, detailing its evaluation method and scientific validation based on global reanalysis products.
